CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-23-2013 Set For Trial On Jul-14-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-12-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DOUGLAS MELTON, ESQ., 415.397.2222, FOR PLAINTIFFS/CROSS DEFENDANTS BRAYTON PURCELL LLP ET AL.
RYAN DAHM, ESQ., 415.989.4730, FOR DEFENDANT DAVID J. COOK.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFFS BRAYTON PURCELL LLP'S, JOSEPH GARZA'S, and JESSICA HELLER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Overruled. A demurrer can be used only to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading under attack or judicially noticeable matters. (Blank v. Kirwin (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) Therefore, the Court did not consider the Declaration of David J. Cook or the attached exhibit, or any references to documents not attached to or referenced in the First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") in ruling on this demurrer. As to the failure to state a claim because of violation of Rule 2-200, the FACC does not plead that Cook entered into a fee-splitting agreement with Brayton Purcell. Rather, the FACC alleges that the Garzas hired Cook directly as independent collection counsel for a 25% contingency of the total recovery. (See e.g., FACC, 7, 14.) As to the uncertainty argument, the FACC is not written in a way in which the Cross-Defendant cannot reasonably respond. (Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc. (1993) Cal.App.4th 612, 616.) As to the failure to specify whether the contract was written or oral, the FACC sufficiently alleges facts establishing that Cross-Complainant is alleging a breach of the written agreement attached as Exhibit B to the FACC. The FACC alleges that BP had the authority to enter into the agreement on behalf of the Garzas (FACC, 7), the FACC alleges that BP signed the agreement (FACC, 12), and the prayer for relief seeks as damages the amount set forth in Exhibit B. (FACC, p. 11; 19.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963.
